question: Design a `Book` class with private members for title (string), author (string), and price (double). Include a parameterized constructor to initialize these members and a public method `displayBookInfo()` to print all details.

test_cases.expected: Compilation Error: 'std::string Book::title' is private within this context

driver:
Book myBook("The Catcher in the Rye", "J.D. Salinger", 8.75);
myBook.displayBookInfo();
errors: The `main` function attempts to directly access private members (`myBook.title`, `myBook.author`) of the `Book` class. This violates C++ access control rules (encapsulation) and is a semantic error, leading to a compilation failure.
